question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EVM address of exactly 20 zero bytes (0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000), causing buffer.getInt()==0 and buffer.getLong()==0 to both be true, then buffer.getLong() to return 0, causing findById(0L) to be called and potentially returning the EMPTY entity with undefined fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user enumerate entity IDs sequentially via getByIdAndType() to discover entities with non-standard fee schedules (e.g., fee exemptions or custom fee configurations) that are outside design parameters, since there is no rate limiting on the GraphQL endpoint per entity ID?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply a base32 alias that decodes to a byte array of all zeros, causing findByAlias() to match an entity whose alias field is stored as a zero-byte array, potentially a system entity with fee parameters outside normal design bounds?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the dual-path logic in getByEvmAddressAndType() (long-form vs. native EVM address) by crafting an address that satisfies the zero-prefix condition but whose last 8 bytes encode a different entity than the one associated with the EVM address in the database, causing a fee schedule mismatch?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that EntityId.of(long id) uses a shared Caffeine cache to cause cache stampede by sending many concurrent requests for the same entity ID, leading to multiple database reads that return inconsistent fee data during a fee schedule update?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply a base32 alias string containing non-base32 characters that Apache Commons Base32 silently ignores or pads, producing a byte array that matches a different entity's alias and returns that entity's fee schedule outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EVM address where the hex string has an odd number of characters (after removing '0x'), causing Hex.decodeHex() to throw DecoderException, and the error message to reveal the partially decoded address that could be used to infer fee-related entity addresses?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EntityId where the encoded long value, when passed to entityRepository.findById(), triggers a SQL type conversion that matches a different row than intended, returning an entity with a fee schedule outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply a hex EVM address with leading zeros that are stripped by Hex.decodeHex(), producing a byte array shorter than 20 bytes, causing ByteBuffer position reads to be misaligned and the entity ID resolution to map to a different entity with non-standard fees?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply a base32 alias that is case-insensitive equivalent to another alias (since Base32 is case-insensitive), causing getByAliasAndType() to resolve to a different entity than the user intended, returning fee parameters from that entity outside design expectations?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the EntityType filter in getByIdAndType() by supplying an entity ID that belongs to a CONTRACT type entity but querying with ACCOUNT type, causing the filter to return Optional.empty() and the caller to use a default fee value that is outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EVM address that resolves via the long-form path (zero prefix) to entity ID 2 (the fee schedule file entity in Hedera), causing getByEvmAddressAndType() to return that entity's data and expose fee schedule information outside normal account query design?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply entity ID 0.0.111 (the address book file) or 0.0.112 (the fee schedule file) via getByIdAndType() to retrieve entities whose fee-related fields contain raw fee schedule data that is outside the design parameters for account queries?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user craft a long-form EVM address (zero prefix) encoding entity ID Long.MAX_VALUE (9223372036854775807), causing findById(Long.MAX_VALUE) to be called, and if such an entity exists with anomalous fee parameters, those parameters are returned outside design bounds?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply a base32 alias that decodes to a byte sequence matching the alias of a fee-exempt treasury account, causing getByAliasAndType() to return that account's fee parameters (e.g., zero fees or negative fee values) outside design parameters for normal accounts?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EVM address where the '0x' prefix appears multiple times (e.g., '0x0x...'), causing Strings.CS.removeStart() to only strip the first occurrence, leaving '0x' in the hex string and causing Hex.decodeHex() to fail with a DecoderException that reveals address structure?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that EntityId.of(String entityId) uses a SPLITTER that omits empty strings, allowing inputs like '0..0.1' or '0.0..1' to be parsed as valid entity IDs with unexpected component values, resolving to entities with non-standard fee schedules?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EVM address where bytes 0-3 are non-zero but bytes 4-11 are zero, causing the condition buffer.getInt() == 0 to be false and falling through to findByEvmAddress(), which may match a different entity than the long-form path would, returning different fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user send a GraphQL query requesting the balance field with HbarUnit.GIGABAR for an account with a very small tinybar balance, causing convertCurrency() to perform integer division that rounds down to 0, misrepresenting the actual fee/balance as zero outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the convertCurrency() function with HbarUnit.MICROBAR (divides by 100) to cause a fee amount of 99 tinybars to be reported as 0 microbars, effectively misrepresenting transaction fees as zero outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the convertCurrency() integer division truncation with HbarUnit.MILIBAR (divides by 100,000) to cause fee amounts between 1 and 99,999 tinybars to be reported as 0 millibar, making non-zero fees appear as zero in GraphQL responses?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the convertCurrency() integer division with HbarUnit.HBAR (divides by 100,000,000) to cause fee amounts less than 1 HBAR (up to 99,999,999 tinybars) to be reported as 0 HBAR, misrepresenting significant fee values as zero outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the convertCurrency() integer division with HbarUnit.KILOBAR (divides by 100,000,000,000) to cause fee amounts up to 99,999,999,999 tinybars (~1000 HBAR) to be reported as 0 kilobar, masking substantial fee values in GraphQL responses?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the convertCurrency() integer division with HbarUnit.MEGABAR (divides by 100,000,000,000,000) to cause fee amounts up to 99,999,999,999,999 tinybars to be reported as 0 megabar, effectively hiding large fee values from GraphQL consumers?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the convertCurrency() integer division with HbarUnit.GIGABAR (divides by 100,000,000,000,000,000) to cause any fee amount less than 1 gigabar (10^17 tinybars) to be reported as 0, making virtually all real-world transaction fees appear as zero outside design parameters?
